REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART, OVERRULED IN PART WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. BREACH OF THE IMPLIED WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, SUPPORTING THAT NOTICE OF THE DILAPIDATIONS WAS GIVEN TO DEFENDANT AND THAT DEFENDANT HAD A REASONABLE OPPORTUNITY TO CURE THE ISSUES. FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION. BREACH OF CONTRACT: THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ATTACHED THE CONTRACT TO HER COMPLAINT AND THEREFORE WAS NOT REQUIRED TO INCORPORATE THE MATERIAL TERMS OF THE CONTRACT INTO THE SAC AS DEFENDANT CONTENDS. (HERITAGE PACIFIC FINANCIAL, LLC V. MONROY (2013) 215 CAL.APP.4TH 972, 993.) ADDITIONALLY, AND CONTRARY TO DEFENDANT'S ASSERTION, THE SAC ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF PERFORMED UNDER THE CONTRACT. (SAC, 39, 40.) SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AS PLED IS SUPERFLUOUS IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S IDENTICAL CLAIM FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. (GUZ V. BECHTEL NATIONAL INC. (2000) 24 CAL.APP.4TH 317, 327.). PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE LEAVE TO ASSERT A NON-DUPLICATIVE CLAIM FOR BREACH OF COVENANT TO GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION. VIOLATION OF S.F. ADMIN. CODE SECTION 37.10B: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. ALL AMENDMENTS SHALL BE ON BOLD FONT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; REPORTED, CARI GONZAGA, CSR#12401, 925-381-5249, CARIWATERS03@YAHOO.COM. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING THE CONCLUSORY ALLEGATION THAT PLAINTIFF SUFFERED EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE PARTIES FAILED TO MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON, OVER THE PHONE OR VIA VIDEO CONFERENCE IN CONJUNCTION WITH THIS DEMURRER. THE COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER ANY FURTHER DEMURRERS/MOTIONS TO STRIKE/MOTIONS FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IF PARTIES FAIL TO COMPLY AGAIN. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION IS DEVOID OF LEGAL ARGUMENT OR CITATIONS TO AUTHORITY, CONSISTING PRIMARILY OF THE BLOCK QUOTATIONS FROM THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F, HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

PROOF OF SERVICE RE: MEMO OF P & A IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS. TO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND DEC. OF THOMAS PURTELL (TRANSACTION ID # 71323046) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MITCHELL, JANEL

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, IN SUPPORT OF MALICE OPPRESSION OR FRAUD. MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER. OPPOSITION IS REVIEWED AND STRICKEN AS UNTIMELY. THE COURT NOTES THAT ON PAGE 7 OF THE OPPOSITION P PURPORTS TO QUOTE PARAGRAPH 44 OF FAC. THE FAC ON FILE WITH THE COURT DOES NOT INCLUDE A PARAGRAPH 44 THAT MATCHES THE QUOTED CONTENTS, WHICH REFERENCE A DEFENDANT "ETHEL CREEK, LLC," "A GWENDOLYN REHLING, AS AN EMPLOYEE AND MANAGING AGENT OF DEFENDANT ETHEL CREEK, LLC" AND A "PLAINTIFF, AN ELDERLY MAN." PARAGRAPH 44 OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED ON SEPTEMBER 13, 2023 READS IN ITS ENTIRETY: "DEFENDANTS KNOWINGLY AND REPEATEDLY MADE THE REPRESENTATION TO PLAINTIFF THAT THE SUBJECT PROPERTY WAS LEGAL AS A RESIDENTIAL DWELLING AND THE SUBJECT PROPERTY WAS HABITABLE FOR PLAINTIFFS." THE PARTIES TO THE ACTION ARE LISTED IN PARAGRAPHS 1 AND 2 OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS "PLAINTIFF JANEL MITCHELL, AN INDIVIDUAL, IS AN ADULT FEMALE INDIVIDUAL" AND "DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING, AN INDIVIDUAL, IS AN ADULT FEMALE INDIVIDUAL ." PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS MOOT AS TO THE SECOND, FOURTH, SIXTH, AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is DENIED in part and moot in part. Motion is moot as to the paragraphs and prayers associated with the cause of action for IIED. Motion is otherwise DENIED, Plaintiff sufficiently states facts re: conscious disregard of Plaintiff's rights.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F, Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with final leave to amend as to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting the conclusory allegation that Plaintiff suffered emotional distress. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. The Court notes that the parties failed to meet and confer in person, over the phone or via video conference in conjunction with this demurrer. The Court will not consider any further demurrers/motions to strike/motions for judgment on the pleadings if parties fail to comply again. The Court further notes that Plaintiff's opposition is devoid of legal argument or citations to authority, consisting primarily of the block quotations from the Third Amended Complaint.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Thomas Purtell Esq., Law offices of Thomas Purtell, 415-722-6291, via zoom for plaintiff
Amanda Bremseth Esq., Lincoln, Gustafson & Cercos, LLP, 619-233-1150, via zoom for defendant

Not contesting tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is moot as to the Second, Fourth, Sixth, and Eighth Causes of action. Defendant's Motion to Strike is DENIED as to the Fifth Cause of Action for breach of contract. Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged a cause of action for breach of a contract that provides for attorney's fees.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reported, Cari Gonzaga, CSR#12401, 925-381-5249, cariwaters03@yahoo.com. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Thomas Purtell Esq., Law offices of Thomas Purtell, 415-722-6291, via zoom for plaintiff
Amanda Bremseth Esq., Lincoln, Gustafson & Cercos, LLP, 619-233-1150, via zoom for defendant

Mr. Purtell contests to the tentative ruling as to 2nd, 4th, 6th and 8th causes of action. Matter discussed. Mr. Purtell requests for 30 days leave to amend. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, OVERRULED in part with 30 days leave to amend. Second Cause of Action. Breach of the Implied Warranty of Habitability: the demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead facts, not conclusions, supporting that notice of the dilapidations was given to Defendant and that Defendant had a reasonable opportunity to cure the issues.
Fourth Cause of Action. Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress: the demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of this cause of action. Fifth Cause of Action. Breach of Contract: the demurrer is OVERRULED. Plaintiff attached the contract to her complaint and therefore was not required to incorporate the material terms of the contract into the SAC as Defendant contends. (Heritage Pacific Financial, LLC v. Monroy (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 972, 993.) Additionally, and contrary to Defendant's assertion, the SAC alleges that Plaintiff performed under the contract. (SAC, 39, 40.) Sixth Cause of Action. Breach of the Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing: the demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. This cause of action as pled is superfluous in light of Plaintiff's identical claim for breach of contract. (Guz v. Bechtel National Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.App.4th 317, 327.). Plaintiff shall have leave to assert a non-duplicative claim for breach of covenant to good faith and fair dealing.
Eighth Cause of Action. Violation of S.F. Admin. Code Section 37.10B: the demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of this cause of action. All amendments shall be on bold font.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reported, Cari Gonzaga, CSR#12401, 925-381-5249, cariwaters03@yahoo.com. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of malice oppression or fraud. Motion to Strike as to the request for attorney's fees is moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Opposition is reviewed and stricken as untimely. The Court notes that on page 7 of the opposition P purports to quote paragraph 44 of FAC. The FAC on file with the Court does not include a paragraph 44 that matches the quoted contents, which reference a Defendant "Ethel Creek, LLC," "a GWENDOLYN REHLING, as an employee and managing agent of Defendant ETHEL CREEK, LLC" and a "Plaintiff, an elderly man." Paragraph 44 of the First Amended Complaint filed on September 13, 2023 reads in its entirety: "Defendants knowingly and repeatedly made the representation to Plaintiff that the subject Property was legal as a residential dwelling and the Subject Property was habitable for Plaintiffs." The parties to the action are listed in paragraphs 1 and 2 of the First Amended Complaint as "Plaintiff Janel Mitchell, an individual, is an adult female individual" and "Defendant GWENDOLYN REHLING, an individual, is an adult female individual ."

Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
